localEmployers.name: Homeboy Industries
localEmployers.address: 130 W Bruno St, Los Angeles, CA 90012
localEmployers.convictionPolicy: Founded specifically for formerly incarcerated and gang-involved. 100% second-chance employer.
localEmployers.notes: THE gold standard for reentry employment. Also provides: tattoo removal, legal services, mental health, substance abuse treatment, job training. Apply in person.

localEmployers.name: Center for Employment Opportunities (CEO)
localEmployers.address: Oakland: 510-251-2240 | San Jose | San Diego | Stockton
localEmployers.convictionPolicy: Exclusively serves people with recent criminal justice involvement.
localEmployers.notes: Start with paid transitional work crew the WEEK you're released. CEO then helps place you in permanent employment. One of the most effective reentry programs in the country.

localEmployers.name: Delancey Street Foundation
localEmployers.address: 600 Embarcadero, San Francisco, CA 94107 | Also in LA
localEmployers.convictionPolicy: 100% second-chance. Run entirely by formerly incarcerated people.
localEmployers.notes: Live-in program: free housing, food, and job training for 2-4 years. You work in their businesses (restaurant, moving company, auto shop) and learn a trade. No cost.

localEmployers.name: Root & Rebound
positions: Free legal help for employment discrimination
localEmployers.convictionPolicy: Helps if you're DENIED a job due to your record. Know your rights under the Fair Chance Act.
localEmployers.notes: If an employer in California asks about your conviction BEFORE a conditional offer, they're breaking the law (AB 1008). Root & Rebound can help you file a complaint.

localEmployers.name: HealthRIGHT 360
localEmployers.convictionPolicy: Actively hires people with lived experience in the justice system.
localEmployers.notes: Healthcare organization that VALUES your lived experience. Peer counselor positions specifically want people who've been through the system.

localEmployers.name: ARC Housing (Los Angeles)
localEmployers.phone: Contact through LA County reentry services
localEmployers.convictionPolicy: Transitional housing with live-in mentors. Employment guidance included.
localEmployers.notes: Combined housing + employment support. Trauma counseling, education, and job placement.

localEmployers.name: LA County Fair Chance Hiring (All County Jobs)
localEmployers.convictionPolicy: LA County is an official Fair Chance Employer. Cannot ask about convictions until after conditional offer.
localEmployers.notes: LA County employs 100,000+ people. Fair chance policy means your record is reviewed AFTER they decide they want to hire you. Also offers WOTC tax credit ($2,400) to employers who hire you.

localEmployers.name: IBEW Local 11 (Electrical Workers Union — LA)
localEmployers.convictionPolicy: Union apprenticeship. Background check is less restrictive than many employers.
localEmployers.notes: 5-year apprenticeship: earn while you learn. Electricians make $80-120K in LA. Union protection. The trades don't care about your past — they care if you show up and work.
